calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To General Interrogatories, Request For Admissions, And Request For Production Of Documents; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against James Sung And E. Reno Cross, In The Sum Of $1200
Off Calendar May-09-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Plaintiff Laura Byrd telephone number 323-239-9893 in Propria Persona.
Reno Cross of Bassi Edlin Huie & Blum LLP telephone number 415-397-9006 for Defendant Golden State Tours.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Laura Byrd's motion to compel discovery responses from James Sung and request for sanctions is denied in its entirety. Mr. Sung has not appeared in this case and is not represented by counsel for any other parties and thus no discovery may be propounded on him as a party. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Laura M. Byrd, in pro per, Tel. 323.239.9893, for plaintiffs;
E. Reno Cross, of Bassi Edlin Huie & Blum LLP, Tel. 415.397.9006, for defendants;

Reno Cross, In The Sum Of $1200 is continued to May 9, 2016, at 9:30 a.m., in Department 302, Law & Motion. Judge Pro Tem to prepare report and recommendation. Judge: David McDonald; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-12-2016 Set For Case Management Conference On May-04-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Golden State Tours, Inc'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

Defendant Golden State Tours, Inc.'s Motion For Summary Judgment - Judge Stewart issues the following tentative ruling: Granted, no opposition filed. If any party contests the Court's ruling, hearing will be held on January 7, 2016 in Department 206 at 9:30 a.m.

The Court did not receive from parties any notice of intent to contest the tentative ruling.
The tentative ruling is adopted: Defendant Golden State Tours, Inc.'s Motion For Summary Judgment is granted. No opposition filed. Prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed form of order and judgment. Plaintiff's CCP 170.3 Challenge as to Judge Goldsmith was not properly served. The CCP 170.3 Challenge is stricken.

Judge: John K. Stewart; Clerk: R. Noguera/D. Lok; Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR#7489
